Scenario.overview: Energy Simple Briefly, the Oldsmar treatment plant intrusion was a real-world attack that occurred in 2021. The attackers targeted the energy sector, exploiting basic vulnerabilities to achieve their goals.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets System Image Target System Artifact Logon Session
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method File Creation (Mechanism) Misconfigured RDP Logon Session Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2013-1347 (Suggested): This vulnerability may corrupt memory in a way that could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code in the context of the current user within Internet Explorer.
stage.description: An employee with poor password hygiene may fall victim to phishing emails sent over the internet, allowing the attackers to gain initial access to the system.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-31956 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows New Technology File System (NTFS) contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows attackers to escalate privileges via a specially crafted application.
stage.capec: CAPEC-555: Remote Services with Stolen Credentials
stage.description: The attackers exploited an outdated software vulnerability in the energy company's internet-facing assets, allowing them to gain further access to the system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2019-3010 (Suggested): Oracle Solaris component: XScreenSaver contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows for privilege escalation.
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers moved laterally through the network due to an unsecured network connection, allowing them to deface the system image.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-21882 (Suggested): Microsoft Win32k contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows for privilege escalation.
stage.description: The attackers exploited a misconfigured RDP connection on an unmonitored machine to gain further access to the system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers compromised the logon session, manipulating settings to disrupt operations at the treatment plant.

Scenario.summary: The attackers exploited basic vulnerabilities in the energy company's systems, gaining initial access through phishing emails and remote desktop software. They then moved laterally through the network, executing malicious actions and ultimately causing disruption to the treatment plant's operations.
